data.name: AssertionFailed
data.value: MuConvert hit an internal error (AssertionFailed). Please report it at https://github.com/MuNet-OSS/MuConvert/issues . ({0})

data.name: InvalidKey
data.value: Invalid key index (must be 1–8): {0}

data.name: InvalidTouchArea
data.value: Invalid touch area specifier: {0}

data.name: NoDuration
data.value: Duration cannot be set on Tap or Touch notes.

data.name: InvalidSlide
data.value: Invalid slide definition: {0}

data.name: MessageAt
data.value: At

data.name: MessageLine
data.value: line {0}

data.name: MessageTime
data.value: bar time {0:W}({1:F2}s)

data.name: MessageParsing
data.value: parsing {0}

data.name: Error
data.value: ERROR:

data.name: Warning
data.value: WARNING:

data.name: Info
data.value: INFO:

data.name: Debug
data.value: DEBUG:

data.name: SimaiGrammarFailed
data.value: Failed to parse simai syntax!

data.name: AbsoluteStepUsed
data.value: The chart uses absolute time step notation like {0}. Support for this feature is experimental; if you run into issues, please report them at https://github.com/MuNet-OSS/MuConvert/issues.

data.name: ExtraModifiersIgnored
data.value: Unexpected modifier(s): {0}. Ignored.

data.name: InvalidWaitTime
data.value: For multi-segment slides with per-segment durations, a wait-time fragment (e.g. {0.5##...}) may only appear on the first segment. Invalid wait-time markers were ignored.

data.name: InstanceMultipleUsage
data.value: Parser/Generator instances are single-use; do not call Parse or Generate repeatedly! Create a new Parser/Generator for each chart you convert.

data.name: InvalidMaidataFile
data.value: Not a valid maidata file: no '&' field markers were found.

data.name: InvalidMA2Sentence
data.value: Unrecognized or invalid MA2 statement!

data.name: InvalidMA2SentenceWarning
data.value: Unrecognized MA2 statement; this line was ignored.

data.name: InvalidMA2HeaderSentenceAfterHeader
data.value: Invalid MA2 statement: after the header section ended, a header-only line appeared again!

data.name: NoBPMInMA2
data.value: The chart has no BPM line; parsing cannot continue!

data.name: UnsuppoertedMA2Version
data.value: This MA2 version is not supported yet. Currently only MA2 1.03–1.05 are supported.

data.name: UnsuppoertedMA2MET_DEF
data.value: Charts whose MET_DEF is not "4 4" are not supported yet!

data.name: NoNotesInChart
data.value: The chart has no notes!

data.name: MA2NoteSentenceTooManyParam
data.value: This MA2 line has more parameters than required; the extras were ignored.

data.name: MA2CNSlideHasWait
data.value: Parse error: MA2 CN___ (connecting slide) has a non-zero wait time.

data.name: MA2CNSlideNoPrevious
data.value: Parse error: MA2 CN___ (connecting slide) has no preceding slide segment!

data.name: StartNoBpm
data.value: No BPM was specified at the start of the chart! A `({0})` has been inserted automatically at the beginning.

data.name: NoteTooNear
data.value: This note is closer than a 384th note to the previous note. MA2 can only represent timing down to 1/384 of a whole note, so these two notes cannot be represented exactly and may be emitted as a yellow EACH pair. If you want a "fake EACH", use syntax such as "1`2", or offset the notes by at least one 384th note.

data.name: PrepFix1
data.value: Attempted to fix {0} syntax error(s) in the chart (inserting possibly missing commas): {1}

data.name: PrepFix2
data.value: Attempted to fix {0} syntax error(s) in the chart (duration markers used "-" instead of ":"): {1}

data.name: PrepFix3
data.value: Attempted to fix {0} syntax error(s) in the chart (star-head modifiers should appear after the lane number and before the star type marker): {1}

data.name: PrepFix4
data.value: Attempted to fix {0} syntax error(s) in the chart (redundant duplicate parentheses, possibly from extra typing): {1}

data.name: VisitFix1
data.value: Attempted to fix a syntax error in the chart (redundant duplicate EACH '/' or fake EACH '`' symbols, possibly from extra typing): {0}

data.name: ExtenedFalseEach
data.value: You used multiple consecutive fake EACH '`' symbols. This is not standard Simai syntax, but following the behavior of other charting tools, it is interpreted as two notes staggered further apart in time (each '`' offsets timing by a 128th note). If this was not intentional, please review your input to avoid mistakes.

data.name: BreakExIn103
data.value: The chart contains features not supported by MA2 1.03 (BreakEx). Those features were ignored and converted back to a pure break.

data.name: BreakHoldOrSlideIn103
data.value: The chart contains features not supported by MA2 1.03 (Break on Hold or Slide). Those features were ignored and converted back to the normal versions.

data.name: ConnectingSlideIn103
data.value: The chart contains features not supported by MA2 1.03 (multi-segment connecting slides). Only the first slide segment was kept.

data.name: AntlrUnknownError
data.value: Simai parser error: {0}

data.name: LexerNoViableAltException
data.value: Unrecognized character: {0} (typo?). Ignored.

data.name: LexerNoViableAltExceptionStrict
data.value: Unrecognized character: {0} (typo?)

data.name: RecoverInlineExtraneousToken
data.value: Extraneous token: {0} (typo?). Ignored.

data.name: RecoverInlineExtraneousTokenStrict
data.value: Extraneous token: {0} (typo?)

data.name: RecoverInlineMissingToken
data.value: Missing token {1} before {0} (typo?). Inserted automatically.

data.name: InputMismatchException
data.value: Unexpected token: {0}. Expected: {1}

data.name: NoViableAltException
data.value: Could not parse chart markup: {0}

data.name: LaxTryfixReminder
data.value: . The affected note(s) was discarded.

data.name: Fixed
data.value: . Fixed automatically.

data.name: FixModifiersOnHead
data.value: For Slide's head, modifiers should appear after the key number and before the slide type notation.
